steps.title: Spot the distance opposition
steps.content: The passage contrasts JWST's location “almost one million miles beyond Earth's orbit” with Hubble's location “only about 340 miles above Earth's surface.” The key signal is only — a word that intensifies how much closer Hubble is, setting up opposition between the two telescope positions.

steps.title: Select the contrast transition
steps.content: By contrast marks opposition between two pieces of information — the transitional function needed here. Therefore falsely suggests cause-and-effect, Similarly wrongly indicates likeness, and Secondly treats this as an independent list item. That matches choice B — B is correct.
